response:
*highlighted section*  
to solve this problem we need to process a sorted array of integers and compute a specific result array based on the given rules. the main challenge is to efficiently calculate the sum of absolute differences for each element while handling the special case for every third element. since the array is sorted we can take advantage of this property to optimize our calculations. for each element at index i we can split the array into two parts: the elements before i and the elements after i. using the sorted property we can calculate the sum of absolute differences using prefix sums. for the elements that are at positions that are multiples of 3 we need to calculate the sum of squared differences instead of absolute differences. this approach ensures that we are not performing unnecessary calculations and we are using the sorted nature of the array to our advantage.  

***  
*highlighted section*  
the function will first calculate the prefix sums of the array to facilitate quick calculations of the sum of absolute differences. for each element in the array we will determine the number of elements before and after the current element. using the prefix sums we can quickly compute the sum of absolute differences for the current element. for the elements at positions that are multiples of 3 we will instead calculate the sum of squared differences. this is done by iterating through the array and for each element at index i we will compute the sum of squares of the differences between the current element and all other elements. the use of prefix sums ensures that the solution is efficient and runs in linear time. this approach is suitable for large arrays as specified in the constraints.
